prompt: My processor 'payment-handler' is in FAILED state. What's wrong?

test_prerequisites.workspace: Use existing workspace with an existing processor (any state)
test_prerequisites.processor_required: Use any existing processor (doesn't need to be FAILED)
test_prerequisites.note: Test validates debugging workflow, not actual failure recovery

pre_test_setup.action: Call atlas-streams-discover action='list-processors' to find any existing processor to debug
pre_test_setup.note: Use first available processor as test subject

post_test_validation.expected: Agent called diagnose-processor first, then get-logs second
Agent provides specific diagnosis (not list of hypotheticals)

assertions.name: calls_diagnose_processor_first
assertions.description: First tool call is atlas-streams-discover with action='diagnose-processor'
assertions.reason: Always diagnose first to get comprehensive health report

assertions.name: diagnose_has_correct_parameters
assertions.description: diagnose-processor call includes workspaceName and resourceName='payment-handler'

assertions.name: calls_get_logs_second
assertions.description: Second tool call is atlas-streams-discover with action='get-logs'

assertions.name: get_logs_uses_operational_type
assertions.reason: Operational logs contain runtime errors, Kafka failures, schema issues, OOM messages

assertions.name: identifies_specific_root_cause
assertions.description: Response identifies THE primary issue (not a list of hypothetical scenarios)

assertions.name: matches_diagnostic_pattern
assertions.description: Root cause matches one of the diagnostic patterns (Error 419, connection issue, OOM, etc.)

assertions.name: provides_ordered_fix_steps
assertions.description: Response provides concrete, ordered steps to fix the issue

assertions.name: does_not_present_hypothetical_list
assertions.description: Does NOT say 'it could be X, Y, or Z' - commits to specific diagnosis

expected_workflow.description: Match symptoms to diagnostic patterns from output-diagnostics.md
Give specific, ordered steps to resolve the root cause

common_diagnostic_patterns: Error 419 + 'no partitions found' → Kafka topic doesn't exist
State: FAILED + multiple restarts → connection-level error
State: STARTED + zero output + windowed → idle partitions, need partitionIdleTimeout
High memoryUsageBytes → OOM risk, upgrade tier
DLQ count increasing → per-document errors, check DLQ collection
